_note: Enrichment seed: techniques used by the named frameworks that were missing from the KB. INSERT-only (new slugs). Apply with scripts/insert-knowledge.mjs. Descriptions kept to ~1 sentence; the Pattern Builder uses the first 160 chars.

techniques.name: Timeboxing
techniques.description: Fixing a hard time limit for a piece of work so that scope and decisions flex to fit the box, rather than the deadline moving.

techniques.name: Backlog Refinement
techniques.description: Ongoing clarifying, splitting and ordering of upcoming backlog items so they are ready before a team commits to them.

techniques.name: Daily Standup
techniques.description: A short daily sync where the team inspects progress toward the goal and surfaces impediments early.

techniques.name: Sprint Review / Demo
techniques.description: A working-software review with stakeholders to inspect the increment and adapt the backlog from real feedback.

techniques.name: Burndown / Burnup Chart
techniques.description: A simple chart tracking remaining or completed work against time to expose whether a goal is on track.

techniques.name: WIP Limits
techniques.description: Capping the number of items allowed in each workflow state to expose bottlenecks and improve flow.

techniques.name: Explicit Workflow Policies
techniques.description: Making the rules for how work moves through each state visible and agreed, including a clear definition of workflow.

techniques.name: Classes of Service
techniques.description: Distinct handling policies for different work types (expedite, fixed-date, standard) to manage urgency and risk.

techniques.name: Service Level Expectation (SLE)
techniques.description: A probabilistic forecast of how long an item will take to flow, used as a delivery commitment.

techniques.name: Throughput / Monte Carlo Forecasting
techniques.description: Forecasting delivery dates probabilistically from historical throughput rather than from estimates.

techniques.name: Replenishment Meeting
techniques.description: A regular cadence to pull the next most valuable items into the committed queue.

techniques.name: Prototyping
techniques.description: Building low-fidelity, often throwaway models of a solution to test understanding and reduce risk early.

techniques.name: Iterative Development and Modelling
techniques.description: Evolving the solution in short iterations with frequent demonstration rather than a single big build.

techniques.name: Facilitated Workshop
techniques.description: A structured, neutrally facilitated session to reach shared decisions quickly across stakeholders.

techniques.name: Prioritised Requirements List
techniques.description: A MoSCoW-prioritised list of requirements that fixes time and cost while flexing scope.

techniques.name: Big Room / PI Planning
techniques.description: A cross-team planning event where all teams plan a shared increment together and surface dependencies face to face.

techniques.name: Scrum of Scrums
techniques.description: A regular cross-team sync where representatives coordinate shared work and resolve inter-team impediments.

techniques.name: Communities of Practice
techniques.description: Cross-team groups that share and align practice in a discipline without central control.

techniques.name: Dependency Mapping / Program Board
techniques.description: Visualising cross-team dependencies and their timing so they can be sequenced or removed.

techniques.name: Multi-team Backlog Refinement
techniques.description: Several teams refining one shared product backlog together to keep a single ordering.

techniques.name: Architectural Runway and Enablers
techniques.description: Building just-enough technical foundation ahead of features so future work can flow without big up-front design.

techniques.name: System Demo
techniques.description: An integrated demo of the whole solution across teams to get aligned feedback each increment.

techniques.name: Inspect and Adapt
techniques.description: A recurring cross-team retrospective and problem-solving workshop on the whole system of delivery.

techniques.name: Portfolio Kanban
techniques.description: Visualising and limiting big initiatives (epics) as they flow through funnel-to-done states at portfolio level.

techniques.name: Minimum Viable Product (MVP)
techniques.description: The smallest thing you can build to test a critical assumption and start learning from real users.

techniques.name: Build-Measure-Learn
techniques.description: The core loop: build an experiment, measure real behaviour, and learn whether to persevere or pivot.

techniques.name: Validated Learning / Innovation Accounting
techniques.description: Measuring progress by evidence of learning about the business model, not by features shipped.

techniques.name: Pivot or Persevere
techniques.description: A scheduled decision, made on evidence, to change direction or keep going on the current hypothesis.

techniques.name: Concierge / Wizard of Oz Test
techniques.description: Manually delivering a service behind the scenes to test demand before building the technology.

techniques.name: Smoke / Landing Page Test
techniques.description: A lightweight test, such as a landing page, that measures intent to buy or sign up before building.

techniques.name: Cohort Analysis
techniques.description: Tracking groups of users over time to read actionable metrics rather than vanity totals.
